transcript: This is British explorer Percy Fawcett. He was last heard from here, deep in the Amazon rainforest, in 1925. He was on his eighth expedition in the Amazon and he had one objective, to find the ruins of a lost city that he called Zee. The idea was based on rumors that had circulated for centuries, that there was once large cities filled with people deep in the Amazon. But Fawcett never found Zee, or any other city. And for decades after his disappearance, experts believed that this rainforest was simply too hostile and too remote to ever have supported cities. Until recently, when scientists began finding these. These ditches and mounds are man-made, and they're all over the rainforest. Now archaeologists think they finally know the answer to an ancient mystery. Where are the lost cities of the Amazon? Here was the capital of the empire.
